name: Sliding shoji door
description: A Japanese-style sliding rice-paper door (shoji) running on a wooden track header and sill. Paired panels of translucent paper stretched over a fine wood lattice slide horizontally past one another, opening up to half the bay clear or, when all panels are pushed aside, the full opening. Used as the primary internal partition between tatami rooms and at the engawa transition where the interior meets the veranda, the shoji softens light, suggests privacy without sealing it, and is removable so two small rooms can merge into one continuous space for gatherings or seasonal reconfiguration.
style_affinity: East-Asian (Japanese/Chinese) motif. Coherent in japanese/chinese (and fantasy). Out of place in Western, Gothic, Renaissance, Modern, Mediterranean buildings — use only if the building is East-Asian.
